steps.title: Identify what the y-intercept represents
steps.content: The $y$-intercept is where the graph crosses the $y$-axis, which means $x=0$. Here $x$ is the number of months after the tablet was purchased. So $x=0$ means the moment it was purchased, before any months have passed.

steps.title: Read the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: At $x=0$, the graph is at $y=225$. So the point is $(0,225)$, meaning the estimated value at purchase was 225 dollars.

steps.title: Interpret in context
steps.content: The credited option says the tablet was worth 225 dollars when it was purchased, which is exactly the meaning of the $y$-intercept. The 24-month value, decrease interpretation, and yearly percentage rate options do not describe the initial value. A is correct.
